prs.title: chore: remove unused dev server config from vite.config.ts
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Summary

Removes the `server` block (port 5173, `/api` proxy) from `vite.config.ts`. This was only used for local `npm run dev` which is not a supported path — the app runs exclusively via Docker (nginx handles the proxy). Only `plugins: [react()]` is needed for the production build.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/2dc8b009513841c6bc24195cc734fb86
Requested by: @AdityaParashar24

prs.title: refactor: remove dead code, unused fields, and orphaned files
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Summary

Removes ~150 lines of dead code across 11 files. Nothing here was called, displayed, or consumed.

**Deleted methods:**
- `DevinClient.create_session()` / `stop_session()` — backend no longer creates or stops sessions (GitHub Action handles session creation)
- `GitHubClient.get_issue_with_comments()` — never called
- `api.getIssue` in frontend — never called from any component

**Deleted fields (stored in DB but never displayed or consumed):**
- `remediation_prompt` — triage session produces it, nothing reads it
- `suggested_validation` — same

**Deleted config:**
- `triage_max_acu` / `remediation_max_acu` in `config.py` + `docker-compose.yml` — ACU limits are set in the GitHub Action YAML, not by the backend

**Deleted files:**
- `backend/prompts/triage.md` and `backend/prompts/remediation.md` — no Python code references these; the GitHub Action constructs prompts independently

**Deleted constants:**
- `LABEL_GENERATED` — defined but never used by the backend

**Kept:** `Stage.APPROVED` — never assigned by the backend, but used by the frontend timeline visualization to show the "Human approval" milestone.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/2dc8b009513841c6bc24195cc734fb86
Requested by: @AdityaParashar24

prs.title: refactor(skills): model plugin-resident skill ownership with OwnerInfo
prs.bodyExcerpt:
Replaces the bespoke `SkillSummary.pluginName` field with the centralized `OwnerInfo` descriptor (`{ kind, id }`) already used by the tool registry, so skill ownership is modeled consistently instead of via a one-off string. The change is internal-only and safe: the client-facing wire field (`pluginName` in `daemon/message-types/skills.ts`) is unchanged and now derived from `owner`, so no mixed-version macOS/platform skew is introduced.

---

- Requested by: @Vargas
- Closes #34121

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/ad2d3cf440094885a18bb84d5321e664
Requested by: @dvargas92495

prs.title: feat(rh): separar ranking de RH em Faltas e Atrasos/Saídas
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Summary

Divide o ranking único de absenteísmo do RH em **dois cards independentes** (empilhados na coluna lateral), cada um com sua própria regra de contagem e ordenação. Ambos continuam recebendo a lista `funcionarios` já escopada pelo filtro global de unidade (`useFuncionarios` filtra por `selected`), então respeitam estritamente a unidade ativa.

Para distinguir falta integral de ausência parcial, o registro de falta ganhou um novo eixo de classificação. Antes `Falta` só tinha `tipo` (com/sem atestado); agora também tem a **categoria da ocorrência** e a **duração** (para parciais):

```ts
type CategoriaFalta = "integral" | "atraso" | "saida_antecipada";
interface Falta {
  id; data;
  tipo: "com_atestado" | "sem_atestado"; // justificativa
  categoria?: CategoriaFalta;            // ausente => "integral" (registros antigos)
  duracaoMinutos?: number;               // Tempo de Ausência (só atraso/saída)
}
```

Sem migration: `faltas` é JSONB; campos novos são opcionais e registros existentes são lidos como `categoria = "integral"`.

**Card 1 — "Ranking de Faltas":** conta apenas `categoria === "integral"`.
**Card 2 — "Ranking de Atrasos e Saídas":** conta `atraso` + `saida_antecipada`, exibe a contagem e a **soma do Tempo de Ausência** (`Σ duracaoMinutos`, formatado em h/min).

Ordenação idêntica nos dois (regra do negócio): `total DESC`, desempate por `semAtestado DESC` (prioriza no topo quem tem mais ocorrências "Sem Atestado/Injustificadas").

```ts
.sort((a, b) => b.total - a.total || b.semAtestado - a.semAtestado)
```

No modal "Detalhes do Funcionário", o formulário de "+ Adicionar Falta" agora tem o seletor **Ocorrência** (Falta Integral / Atraso / Saída Antecipada) e, quando parcial, um campo **Tempo de Ausência (min)**. A lista do histórico mostra a categoria e a duração ao lado do badge de atestado.

## Test plan
- `tsc --noEmit` e `npm run build` passando.
- Registrar no modal uma Falta Integral, um Atraso (com minutos) e uma Saída Antecipada (com m

prs.title: refactor(plugins): thread post-compact re-injection result on the hook context
prs.bodyExcerpt:
Reshapes `postCompact` from a value-returning function into the threaded-context shape that `runHook` requires: it now mutates `ctx.history` in place and returns `void` (typed as `PluginHookFn<PostCompactContext>`), and the agent loop reads the re-injected history back off the context instead of a return value. This is the contract change that lets post-compaction re-injection become a registered `post-compact` hook (a later step) rather than a direct call.

It's safe because behavior is unchanged — the loop still passes the same inputs and consumes the same re-injected messages; only the plumbing (mutate-in-place vs. return) changes, mirroring how the memory `user-prompt-submit` hook already overwrites `latestMessages`. The injection blocks the transform captures were already dropped by the only caller, so nothing is lost by propagating just the messages.

---

- Requested by: @vargas@vellum.ai
- Session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/59e1d9bfdd584bcb9571addbcd80a056

Requested by: @dvargas92495

Removes the experimental `--no-compile` build path now that the compiled-binary path is the only one we ship: drops the `no_compile` workflow_dispatch input and its conditional steps from the Dev Release workflow, and deletes the associated dead machinery in `clients/macos/build.sh` (runtime staging, launcher generation, native-addon signing, and symlink-dereferencing helpers). The default `--compile` path is untouched, so dev releases build exactly as before.

prs.title: feat(schema): mark toolCalls/surfaces @deprecated in favor of contentBlocks
prs.bodyExcerpt:
Marks the conversation `toolCalls` and `surfaces` arrays `@deprecated` on `ConversationMessageSchema`, since each is already represented unconditionally by a `contentBlocks` variant (`tool_use`/`surface`). This continues the deprecation set toward the unified `contentBlocks` projection (following `content` in #34122 and the positional arrays in #34071); the fields keep their existing shape and are still emitted, so it's annotation-only with no wire impact. `attachments` is intentionally left non-deprecated because `contentBlocks` does not yet have attachment parity — orphan/unmatched file-block refs ship only via the flat `attachments` array (see `alignAttachments`), so deprecating it now would mislead clients into dropping those chips.

---

- Requested by: @dvargas92495
- Session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/dbd1c2f16a544f9bb42876e5e25f9f1a

prs.title: Fix Supabase env config so app renders on localhost
feat: Phase 1-3 marketing page updates — dynamic stats, new features, presentation, glossary
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Summary

All marketing pages had hardcoded subcontractor counts that were stale (18K/78K/200+ when real count is 175K+/45) and 9 features shipped in PR #649-660 had zero marketing page presence. This PR fixes all three phases in one commit.

### Phase 1: Dynamic stats (credibility fix)

New `useNetworkStats()` hook fetches live counts from `master_subcontractors` with a 5-minute `sessionStorage` cache:

```tsx
// src/landing/hooks/useNetworkStats.ts
const [totalRes, emailRes, smallBizRes, verifiedRes] = await Promise.all([
  supabase.from('master_subcontractors').select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true }),
  // ... 3 more parallel queries
])
```

- **LandingPage**: Both stat blocks (hero + network section) now render `stats.total.toLocaleString()` instead of hardcoded `18,000+` / `78,000+`
- **ForSubcontractorsPage**: `LiveStats()` now uses the shared hook; fallback `|| 200` → `|| 45` for trade categories
- **ExploreNetworkPage**: Loading skeleton (`animate-pulse` placeholder) while stats load — no more "0+" flash

### Phase 2: New feature marketing (PR #649-660)

- **ProductPage**: 4 new module entries (Radius Matching, AI SOW Mapping, RFQ Composer, Org Sub DB) → 14→18 modules
- **HowItWorksPage**: 6→8 steps — added "Match Subs by Location & Trade" and "Compose & Send RFQs" steps
- **SolutionsPage**: Each industry's feature list updated with radius matching, RFQ composer, AI quote compliance
- **LandingPage**: Network section features updated (radius search, SOW mapping, RFQ composer instead of generic descriptions)

### Phase 3: Polish

- **Presentation**: 6 new slides (Radius Matching, SOW Mapping, RFQ Composer, Org Sub DB, Contactability, Complete Flow) + all `18,000+` → `175,000+`, `200+` → `45+`
- **SolutionsPage**: Industry Terms Glossary section (FAR, DFARS, SOW, NAICS, RFP, RFQ, SAM.gov, CUI, FedRAMP)
- **App.tsx**: `/create-account` → redirect to `/login?tab=signup`
- **StatusPage**: 10-second `AbortController` timeout on health endpoint fetch

Link to

prs.title: Fix balance, fees, send UX, and settings actions (v1.6.3)
prs.bodyExcerpt: ## Summary Addresses user feedback for balance accuracy, fixed send fees, post-send navigation, and new settings actions. ## Changes (v1.6.3) ### Balance fix - Dashboard now shows **explorer-confirmed balance** (sum of all receive addresses via API) instead of potentially stale local UTXO sum - Auto-rebuilds UTXOs from explorer when local balance mismatches network - Balance text color changed to **white** ### Fixed send fees | Tier | Fee | |------|-----| | Standard | 0.0001 2X2 | | Fast | 0.0002 2X2 | | Priority | 0.004 2X2 | ### Send UX - After successful send: clears address/amount fields and navigates back to **Dashboard** automatically ### Settings — Reindex blockchain - Deletes local blocks, transactions, and UTXO balance - Re-downloads chain data and re-syncs from explorer - Wallet keys are preserved ### Settings — Delete wallet - Confirmation dialog warns about **permanent data loss** - Clears all local wallet data and returns to wallet creation screen ## Testing - `./gradlew assembleDebug` — BUILD SUCCESSFUL - APK: `app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug-1.6.3.apk`

prs.title: Fix cleanmgr hang and add multi-profile cache cleanup for LocalSystem runs
prs.bodyExcerpt: ## Problem Two issues when running via RMM as LocalSystem: 1. `cleanmgr /sagerun` hung permanently — `$isAdmin` is `$true` for LocalSystem, so the guard didn't exclude it. `cleanmgr.exe` requires an interactive desktop session; under LocalSystem its cleanup handlers block indefinitely. 2. Profile cache cleanup was entirely skipped with a "Skipping" message, leaving temp files and browser caches for all local user accounts untouched. ## Changes **Fix cleanmgr hang (`Invoke-Main`, `Invoke-CleanMgrSageRun`)** - Guard changed from `if ($isAdmin)` to `if ($isAdmin -and -not $isSystem)` so cleanmgr is skipped entirely under LocalSystem. - `Start-Process -Wait` replaced with `$proc.WaitForExit(300000)` + `$proc.Kill()` fallback — 5-minute ceiling prevents hang during interactive admin runs. **Multi-profile cache cleanup (`Get-CleanableUserProfile`, `Clear-UserProfileCache`)** - `Get-CleanableUserProfile` enumerates profiles via `Get-CimInstance Win32_UserProfile`, filtering out: service/system accounts (`Special = $true`), currently logged-on users (`Loaded = $true`), and roaming/network profiles (UNC `LocalPath`). - `Clear-UserProfileCache` cleans per profile: `AppData\Local\Temp`, thumbnail cache, Edge/Chrome (Default + all named `Profile *` subdirectories), and Firefox `cache2` directories. - The former "Skipping profile caches" `else`-branch is replaced with the enumeration loop. ## Testing - ✅ Syntax check: `ParseFile` reports no errors - ✅ PSScriptAnalyzer: no new warnings introduced (only pre-existing warnings remain)

prs.title: fix(ux): Lade-Feedback, Scroll-Fix und Performance
prs.bodyExcerpt: ## Zusammenfassung Behebt das Gefühl von „reagiert nicht“ bei Klicks und das fehlende Mausrad-Scrolling auf der Startseite. ## Änderungen ### Scroll-Fix - Entfernt verschachtelten Scroll-Container in `.site-main` — die Seite scrollt jetzt natürlich über das Dokument - Mausrad funktioniert überall (auch über Header/Footer-Bereich) - `overflow: hidden` am Chrome-Header entfernt ### Lade-Feedback - **`SubmitButton`** mit Spinner und Text (z. B. „Kampf startet…“) für alle Formulare - **`NavigationProgress`** — dünne Fortschrittsleiste oben bei Link-Klicks und Formular-Absendungen - **`StartPlayingButton`** und **`SignOutButton`** mit Spinner und `aria-busy` - Sofortiges `:active`-Feedback bei Button-Klicks ### Performance - Gegner-Seite: parallele Abfrage von Spieler-Loadout und Gegner-IDs - Landing-Demo-Kampf in `landing-demo.ts` ausgelagert (einmal berechnet) ## Testplan - [x] `npm run typecheck` - [x] `npm run lint` - [ ] Startseite mit Mausrad scrollen - [ ] „Herausfordern“ — Spinner + Fortschrittsleiste sichtbar - [ ] „Jetzt spielen“ — Ladezustand bis Dashboard - [ ] Deck-Slots ausrüsten/entfernen — Feedback sichtbar

prs.title: Enterprise FLYHUB Flight Booking System with Pixel-Perfect UI

prs.title: Fix release artifact publishing
prs.bodyExcerpt: ### Summary - Split release builds from publishing so macOS and Windows jobs no longer race to create the same GitHub release. - Upload platform build outputs as workflow artifacts and publish them from a single final job. - Preserve the existing release asset set: macOS `.dmg` + `.app.tar.gz`, Windows `.msi` + `.exe`. ### Testing - `go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@latest .github/workflows/release.yml`

prs.title: Fix bulk strategy delete network error
prs.bodyExcerpt: ## Problem Bulk delete showed a generic "Network error" because: 1. `?uid=` was missing from the fetch URL (session auth middleware expects query/header UID like single delete) 2. Deleting many strategies via ORM cascade timed out on Railway/gunicorn ## Fix - Add `?uid=` + `credentials: same-origin` on bulk-delete requests - Fast bulk SQL: DELETE executions/performance/competition rows in one sweep - Run DB work in `asyncio.to_thread`; invalidate strategies cache - UI deletes in chunks of 20 with progress label and real error messages

prs.title: Always use max_completion_tokens via shared chat completion helper.
prs.bodyExcerpt: Centralize OpenAI requests so PM agent and agentic loops never send deprecated max_tokens after the main merge.

prs.title: Fix cleanmgr hang under LocalSystem in DiskCleanup.ps1
prs.bodyExcerpt: ## Problem When run via RMM as LocalSystem, the script hung indefinitely at the `cleanmgr /sagerun` step. Two root causes: 1. `$isAdmin` is `$true` for LocalSystem, so the cleanmgr block was not guarded against the LocalSystem context. `cleanmgr.exe` requires an interactive desktop session; under LocalSystem several of its cleanup handlers block on COM/UI calls that never complete. 2. `Start-Process -Wait` has no timeout, so a stalled `cleanmgr.exe` process stalled the entire script permanently. ## Changes - **`Invoke-Main`:** Added `-and -not $isSystem` to the cleanmgr guard so the block is skipped entirely when running as LocalSystem. - **`Invoke-CleanMgrSageRun`:** Replaced `-Wait` with `$proc.WaitForExit(300000)` (5-minute ceiling). If cleanmgr does not exit within that window the process is force-killed and a warning is emitted. This protects interactive admin runs where cleanmgr might still misbehave. ## Testing - ✅ Syntax check: `ParseFile` reports no errors - ✅ PSScriptAnalyzer: no new warnings introduced (pre-existing warnings unchanged)

prs.title: Refactor About Gridly into mobile-friendly nested accordions
prs.bodyExcerpt:
### Motivation
- Improve the mobile reading experience by replacing a long, stacked About Gridly content block with scan-friendly expandable sections that match the existing Settings accordion UX. 
- Preserve all existing About Gridly copy and visual premium treatment while reducing scrolling and keeping tap targets readable on portrait mobile. 

### Description
- Replace the stacked `article.settings-about-gridly-block` content with nested `<details class="settings-about-gridly-topic">` sections in the static markup (`index.html`) and the runtime generator (`buildGridlyAboutGuidanceHtml()` in `js/app.js`).
- Rename the last row from "How Gridly Works" to "How Trust Works" and add the requested Mission paragraph to the Mission section while keeping the original copy intact. 
- Add new accordion styling and portrait adjustments in `css/styles.css` under V272.4 to reuse the Settings expand/collapse visual language and provide open-state affordances for nested topics. 
- Presentation-only change that leaves Settings actions, build/version display, feedback controls, and all app logic untouched. 

### Testing
- Ran `node --check js/app.js` to validate the modified JS and it passed. 
- Executed a `python3` content-presence check that verified the five topic labels and the additional Mission paragraph exist in both `index.html` and `js/app.js`, and it passed. 
- Ran `git diff --check` and `git status --short` to confirm no diff errors and a clean index state, and both checks passed. 
- Headless browser tooling (Playwright / local browser) was not available in this environment, so visual QA should be completed on a portrait mobile device following the provided phone testing steps.

------

prs.title: Use numeric Supabase migration versions
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Summary
- rename cloud auth migration to 0040 and telemetry migration to 0041 so both are numeric and sort before 005
- add a guard test requiring numeric Supabase migration version prefixes

## Why
Supabase CLI rejects non-numeric migration versions, and 0041 sorted before 004_cloud_auth_users.sql by filename. Numeric 0040/0041 keeps migration history valid and ordered.

## Tests
- /Users/kartikraghavan/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/python/bin/python3 -m unittest tests.test_supabase_migrations tests.test_candidate_facts_supabase_store tests.test_candidate_facts_repository

prs.title: Move GPIO, Wi-Fi, and MQTT settings to Kconfig
prs.bodyExcerpt:
### Motivation
- Make hardware pin assignments and network/MQTT parameters configurable via `menuconfig` instead of hardcoded `#define` constants to simplify reconfiguration per board/deployment. 
- Avoid embedding Wi‑Fi credentials and broker settings in source files by exposing them as build-time `CONFIG_*` options. 
- Centralize GPIO defaults so sensor and LED pin mappings can be adjusted without editing component sources. 

### Description
- Added `main/Kconfig.projbuild` which defines `CONFIG_TEMP_SENSORS_*` options for DHT/BH1750/LED GPIO pins, Wi‑Fi SSID/password and MQTT broker/topic with existing values set as defaults. 
- Replaced hardcoded Wi‑Fi and MQTT macros in `main/main.c` with `CONFIG_TEMP_SENSORS_WIFI_SSID`, `CONFIG_TEMP_SENSORS_WIFI_PASSWORD`, `CONFIG_TEMP_SENSORS_MQTT_BROKER_URL` and `CONFIG_TEMP_SENSORS_MQTT_TOPIC`, and added `#include "sdkconfig.h"` where needed. 
- Replaced fixed GPIO `#define`s in `components/dht_component/dht_component.c`, `components/bh1750_component/bh1750_component.c` and `components/led_component/led_component.c` with `((gpio_num_t)CONFIG_TEMP_SENSORS_...)` values and added `#include "sdkconfig.h"` in those files. 
- Updated `README.md` to document the new `menuconfig` options and default values. 

### Testing
- Ran `git diff --cached --check` and `git show --check HEAD --` to validate no whitespace/check issues, both completed without errors. 
- Searched source files to confirm replacements (no remaining hardcoded Wi‑Fi/MQTT/pin `#define` usages in `main` and modified components). 
- Attempted `idf.py --version` to check IDF availability but `idf.py` is not present in this environment so a full build could not be executed here.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_6a286d9f57948330999b71d21ab4a6d0)

prs.title: Support OPENAI_API_KEY_FILE for local dev, update docs/.env.example, and harden page chrome
prs.bodyExcerpt:
### Motivation

- Make local development safer and easier by allowing an API key to be loaded from a local file instead of pasting secrets into environment variables. 
- Provide clear local-run instructions and ship a non-secret ` .env.example` to demonstrate the recommended setup. 
- Improve UI resiliency so basic navigation (tabs/theme) works even if non-critical page chrome scripts fail in static previews or local-file contexts.

### Description

- Add a new ` .env.example` showing `OPENAI_API_KEY_FILE` usage and update `README.md` with Windows-friendly example paths and `npm install && npm start` instructions. 
- Update `.gitignore` to exclude `API.txt` and `*.key` to reduce the chance of committing secrets. 
- Implement `parseOpenAIKeyFile` and `loadOpenAIKeyFromFile` in `server.js` which read and parse a key or `OPENAI_API_KEY=...` line from the file referenced by `OPENAI_API_KEY_FILE` and set `process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY` when present. 
- Refactor page chrome in `cap_cloze_refiner.html` to isolate tab activation, consolidate theme handling, wrap non-critical UI initialization in a `try/catch`, and improve messaging for synoptic iframe theme posting. 
- Adjust `script.js` to prefer a local backend when the page is loaded as a file, introduce `localApiOrigin` and `deployedApiOrigin` variables, and clarify the static backend message to instruct running the local server when appropriate. 

### Testing

- No automated tests were run for this change.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_6a2850ff5dd4832b8fc0232fb1fcd2ef)

prs.title: Add configurable world templates
Admin route & modal refactor; preserve likes/votes after board end; protect objective behind admin
prs.bodyExcerpt:
### Motivation
- Separar el flujo de administración del de participantes para permitir un acceso directo vía `/admin` sin mostrar elementos de participante y simplificar el proceso de toma de control por PIN. 
- Hacer que el tablero quede cerrado para nuevas tarjetas pero que las interacciones de votación (me gusta / votos de acciones) sigan disponibles tras el fin del tiempo. 
- Evitar que usuarios no-admin modifiquen el objetivo del sprint en el servidor.

### Description
- Añade `ADMIN_ROUTE` y marca `body.is-admin-route` para ocultar elementos de participante (`#music-bar`, `#participant-dashboard`, `#retro`, `#mini-race`) y oculta el botón de acceso admin en la UI con CSS. 
- Simplifica el modal de admin para que solicite solo el `PIN` y elimina campos de identidad/personaje del flujo admin; el admin ya no crea/ajusta `currentPilot` desde el modal. 
- Cambia la lógica cliente/servidor para permitir likes/votos aun cuando el tablero haya terminado, ajusta textos que informan que el tablero está "cerrado", y actualiza estilos para no deshabilitar botones de like cuando `board-ended`. 
- Protege la ruta de guardado del objetivo usando `adminFetch` en el cliente y añadiendo `requireAdmin` en el endpoint `POST /api/objective`, además de prevenir que usuarios no-admin intenten guardar desde la UI. 
- Varias mejoras de accesibilidad/UX en controles del cronómetro (`aria-label` / `title`) y pequeños ajustes de placeholders/labels.

### Testing
- Se ejecutó la suite de tests automatizados (`tests/server.test.js`) y los nuevos casos añadidos pasaron correctamente. 
- Nuevos tests incluidos: `Admin puede guardar objetivo sin perder pasos`, `POST /api/objective rechaza sin credenciales admin`, y `Votos de tarjetas y acciones siguen habilitados con tiempo terminado`, y todos ellos completaron con éxito. 
- Test modificado: `POST /api/cards rechaza cuando terminó el tiempo del tablero` actualizado para esperar el nuevo mensaje que indica que el tablero "queda cerrado" y pasó

prs.title: Remove Phase 5–10 Python env/obs code, configs and tests; consolidate observation adapters
prs.bodyExcerpt:
### Motivation
- Phase 4 recurrent MAPPO is the active branch; phase 5–10 Python-only env wrappers, observation adapters, probe configs and related tests are being removed to reduce maintenance surface while keeping Phase 4 and Phase 11 functionality intact.

### Description
- Deleted legacy phase env wrappers: `python/envs/phase5_entity_mappo.py`, `phase6_grid_mappo.py`, `phase7_fog_mappo.py`, `phase8_random_map_mappo.py`, `phase9_snapshot_mappo.py`, and `phase10_target_slot_mappo.py` while keeping `phase11_current_selfplay_mappo.py` and all Phase-4 envs.
- Removed the separate `entity_obs.py` and `grid_obs.py` modules and folded/rewired the necessary constants and helpers into `multi_enemy_obs.py` and `partial_obs.py` (so existing Phase-4/11 callers still resolve the required symbols via the kept adapters).
- Deleted `python/train/entity_attention.py` only if not referenced (kept when in use); module-level grep and imports were audited and preserved when still referenced by trainer code (no silent removals of used modules).
- Updated registries and factories to remove phase5–10 entries: `python/envs/__init__.py`, `python/envs/runtime_factory.py`, `python/train/phases.py`, and `python/train/runtime_specs.py` now only expose/resolve Phase 4 and Phase 11 legacy paths and the explicit runtime API; removed PHASE_REGISTRY entries for 5–10.
- Deleted probe/config YAMLs for phases 5–10 under `experiments/configs/` while keeping `experiments/configs/phase11/` and all `phase4/` configs untouched.
- Removed tests which exercised the deleted env wrappers: `python/tests/test_phase5_entity_obs.py`, `test_phase6_grid_obs.py`, `test_phase10_target_slot.py`, and excised the parts of other tests that were solely about the removed phases; kept tests that exercise adapters/utilities still in use (partial obs, map randomization, snapshot helpers, phase11 and phase4 tests remain).
- Adjusted imports across the codebase to reference the consolidated `multi_enemy_obs` / `partial_obs` sym

prs.title: profile-feed: wire identity card user label and age to i18n
prs.bodyExcerpt:
### Motivation
- The profile identity card in `/profile-feed` used hardcoded Spanish strings for the user label and age, so these needed to be sourced from the existing i18n system to support CO/US locales dynamically without changing layout or backend logic.

### Description
- Added a new translation key `profileFeedAge` to `lib/i18n.ts` with CO value `"{age} Años"` and US value `"{age} years old"` for dynamic age formatting.
- Extended `components/profile-feed/ProfileIdentityCard.tsx` with two presentation props: `userLabel` and `formatAge`, replacing the hardcoded `usuario` label and `age` rendering while keeping existing defaults intact.
- Updated `app/profile-feed/page.tsx` to pass `userLabel={t("profileFeedUser").toLocaleUpperCase(locale)}` and `formatAge={(value) => interpolate(t("profileFeedAge"), { age: value })}` so the card renders `USUARIO`/`USER` and `X Años`/`X years old` according to locale.

### Testing
- Ran `npm run lint` which finished with 0 errors and the repository's existing 15 `@next/next/no-img-element` warnings (no new lint errors).
- Started the dev server and performed `curl -I http://localhost:3000/profile-feed`, receiving `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`.
- Verified whitespace/format checks via `git diff --check` which returned clean.

prs.title: fix: preserve Portfolio Plan Board timeline scroll positions during epic selection
fix: use AR invoice txnDate as receipt date instead of payment file date
prs.bodyExcerpt:
Receipt payloads (`ReceiptDate`, `AccountingDate`, `DepositDate`, `GlDate`) were sourced from the Vend payment Excel date column, which can differ from the matched AR invoice's `txnDate` by a day — receipts showed `2025-03-24` while the invoice date was `2025-03-25`.

## Changes

- **`findInvoiceHeader`** — returns `invoiceDate` extracted from the matched `FusionInvoiceHeader.txnDate`, formatted as `YYYY-MM-DD` UTC. Includes an `isNaN` guard for malformed dates.
- **`generateReceipts` payload builder** — introduces `receiptDate = invoiceInfo.invoiceDate || date` and applies it to all date fields in both standard and misc receipt payloads. Falls back to the payment file date only when no invoice date is available.

```js
// Before — date from payment Excel
ReceiptDate: date,
AccountingDate: date,

// After — date from matched AR invoice, payment date as fallback
const receiptDate = invoiceInfo.invoiceDate || date;
ReceiptDate: receiptDate,
AccountingDate: receiptDate,
```

prs.title: Fix ClientDesk manual update flow for metadata-based releases
prs.bodyExcerpt:
Plugin page updates regressed after the release format changed from a directly-installed ZIP to metadata-driven releases. WordPress still showed an update notice, but the ClientDesk “Update Now” path did not execute a valid plugin upgrade.

- **What changed**
  - The manual updater now resolves either:
    - a direct ZIP URL, or
    - a `clientdesk-update.json` metadata URL that contains the actual package URL and version
  - The update path now performs a real plugin upgrade via `Plugin_Upgrader::upgrade()` instead of using install semantics.

- **Update path hardening**
  - Added `raw.githubusercontent.com` to the allowed package hosts so GitHub-hosted release packages are accepted.
  - Seeded the plugin update transient with the resolved package/version before invoking the upgrader, so the update runs through the standard WordPress plugin-upgrade flow.

- **Operator feedback**
  - Added explicit plugin-page notices for:
    - successful update
    - failed update with surfaced error message
    - local-mode blocked update
    - no update available

- **Release metadata**
  - Bumped ClientDesk to `2.9.23`
  - Updated `clientdesk-update.json` to point at `clientdesk-v2_9_23.zip`
  - Rebuilt the packaged release zip

```php
$resolved_update = $this->resolve_update_package( $latest, $url );

$current->response[ $plugin_file ] = (object) [
    'plugin'      => $plugin_file,
    'new_version' => $resolved_update['latest'],
    'package'     => $resolved_update['url'],
];

$upgrader = new Plugin_Upgrader( new WP_Ajax_Upgrader_Skin() );
$result   = $upgrader->upgrade( $plugin_file );
```

prs.title: Add layer-aware interactive HTML graph controls
prs.bodyExcerpt:
The generated HTML graph was too dense to explain cross-layer relationships quickly. This update reshapes the graph around a simpler layered view: start from Frontend, expand toward Backend/Android and BD, and filter the view down to only the entities and dependencies that matter.

- **Layered graph model**
  - Classifies rendered nodes into `Frontend`, `Backend`, `Android`, and `BD`.
  - Switches the HTML view to a left-to-right hierarchical layout so the default reading path is `Frontend → Backend/Android → BD`.
  - Uses layer color as the primary visual cue while keeping node shape for technical type.

- **Interactive graph controls**
  - Adds per-layer checkboxes to show/hide full layers.
  - Adds search to match domain entities, screens, buttons, endpoints, and packages such as `Pkg_productos`.
  - Search narrows the graph to matches plus directly related nodes, hiding unrelated noise.
  - Adds double-click expand/collapse on nodes to reveal dependencies incrementally instead of rendering the full dependency surface at once.

- **Renderer changes**
  - Replaces the previous PyVis-generated default HTML shell with a custom `vis-network` template.
  - Serializes node metadata needed by the client view (`layer`, `searchText`, key-node flags, sizing, colors).
  - Filters out invalid edges during render so the HTML only contains navigable relationships.

- **Coverage and docs**
  - Adds renderer-focused tests for controls, layer inference, label truncation, node serialization, and missing-edge filtering.
  - Updates README copy to describe the new layered HTML interaction model.

```python
graph.add_node(
    RouteNode(
        id="backend:endpoint",
        label="GET /productos",
        kind=NodeKind.ENDPOINT,
        file="api/app.py",
        metadata={"framework": "flask"},
    )
)

graph.add_node(
    RouteNode(
        id="db:pkg",
        label="Pkg_productos",
        kind=NodeKind.SQL,
        file="db/packages.sql",
    )
)
```

This produces HTML where

Redesign share extension UI to match Apple Notes style
prs.bodyExcerpt:
The share extension used a generic `Form`-based picker UI. This replaces it with a layout matching the native Apple Notes share sheet: custom chrome header, destination row with chevron, and a combined content input area showing the shared content inline.

## Layout changes (`SharePickerView`)
- **Header bar** — circular `×` dismiss button, app name title, yellow capsule Save button (`.foregroundStyle(.black)` to stay legible in dark mode)
- **"SAVE TO" section** — tappable destination row (`New Note` or existing note title + chevron) with a contextual subtitle; tapping opens a `NavigationStack` picker sheet listing all existing notes for append mode
- **Content input area** — single rounded-rect container with a `TextEditor` (placeholder: "Add text to note...") above a divider, then either an `LPLinkView` URL card (via `LinkPresentation`) or a plain text preview below

## `LinkPreviewCard` (`UIViewRepresentable`)
Wraps `LPLinkView` with async metadata fetch via `LPMetadataProvider`. Uses `[weak linkView]` in the completion handler and cancels the in-flight fetch in `Coordinator.deinit` to avoid crashes on early dismissal.

## Data changes
`onSave` now carries `additionalText`; `saveNote` combines it with the shared content (`additionalText + "\n\n" + content`) before writing to the App Group `UserDefaults`. No changes needed in the main app's `importPendingSharedNotes`.

prs.title: feat(tech): add Aider polyglot to benchmark leaderboards
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## What

Adds **Aider polyglot** as a fourth benchmark on the 技術 tab, following the leaderboard-credibility research.

## Why this one

The brief's audience builds agents/coding tooling, and Aider's polyglot leaderboard (225 Exercism code-editing exercises across C++/Go/Java/JS/Python/Rust) is a credible, widely-cited coding-agent ranking. Crucially, it's the only researched candidate with a **stable, official, machine-readable** endpoint:

| candidate | credible? | machine-readable endpoint | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Aider polyglot** | yes | ✅ official raw-GitHub YAML, current | **added** |
| LMArena | yes (highest value) | ⚠️ official HF space = 2-yr-stale pickle; live data only via unofficial scraper | deferred |
| LiveCodeBench | yes | ⚠️ client-side-rendered (the MTEB/PinchBench failure mode) | deferred |
| Artificial Analysis | yes | ❌ no free machine-readable endpoint | skipped |
| HF Open LLM Leaderboard | — | ❌ discontinued 2025 | skipped |

## How

- `leaderboards-parsers/aider.js`: fetch `polyglot_leaderboard.yml`, keep the best `pass_rate_2` per model (models have multiple runs), rank descending.
- Register the `aider` parser + a `leaderboard-aider` source (registry now 52 sources).
- Add the canonical url (`aider.chat/docs/leaderboards/`) to `leaderboard-urls.js` and teach `benchOf` the "aider" title token, so the merge cure stamps the right link.
- tech curator: add Aider polyglot to the active-benchmark list.

## Tests

Parser unit tests (rank/dedupe/drop-bad/non-list) + `benchOf` + registry count.

- `npm test` → 384 passed (60 files)
- `npm run lint` → clean
- **Live smoke-test**: fetched the real YAML → 68 models parsed, `gpt-5 (high)` #1.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

prs.title: [DOCS] research: designer-first dispatch (#1084)
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Summary

Research-only survey for #1084. The orchestrator today hard-wires every spawn to the implementer template; some work items (audits, `[RESEARCH-DELTA]` spec amendments, wedge ideation) need a designer pass first so the brief lands before code. This document covers prior art, regatta-side router signals, the precise code seam, and the failure modes a router has to defend against.

- Prior art: Aider v0.83.0 (Apache-2.0), OpenHands v0.31.x (MIT), SWE-agent v0.7.x (MIT). Recurring pattern: finite enum of role names + one explicit signal (operator command / config / label) + sequenced via graph or operator. Sweep (archived, LLM-classified) is the anti-pattern.
- Signals: `WorkItem.Kind`, GitHub labels via `spec_adapter.selector`, title prefix (`idPrefixRE` at `parse.go:38`), `## Dispatch sequence` body H2, file-scope hint, GitHub native `issueType`. Layered priority (body > label > title-prefix).
- Seam: `parseIssueBody` populates `WorkItem.DispatchRoles`; scheduler `dispatch` step picks the next-uncommitted role; spawner stays role-agnostic via existing `ClaudeSpawnerConfig.Prompt`.
- Failure modes: label drift, body-heading typo, conflicting signals, designer deliverable detection, empty `DispatchRoles`, role enum drift, re-route on retry. Silent fallthrough is the dominant trap.

No design decisions committed; next step is a designer pass that produces a spec under `docs/engineer/specs/`.

## Test plan

- [x] `bash scripts/doc-check.sh` clean (banned-phrase + link gates).
- [x] `bash scripts/check-phase-x-leak.sh` clean (no phase-x tokens in doc).
- [x] `bash scripts/check-doc-links.sh` clean.
- [x] All cited repo paths (`internal/orchestrator/spawner/claude.go`, `internal/orchestrator/scheduler/scheduler.go`, `internal/orchestrator/adapter/githubissues/parse.go`, `contracts/schemas/spec_adapter.go`, `docs/engineer/dispatch-templates/{designer,implementer,triage,reviewer}.md`, `docs/engineer/briefs/2026-06-07-research-loop-gap-bridge.md`) verified via `git

prs.title: feat: add Continue and Aider developer-AI connector parse surfaces
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Summary

Adds two developer-AI tool connectors as read-only parse surfaces, capturing developer-AI **decision and implementation evidence**. Each maps a provider artifact to a provider-neutral `Observation` feeding `adapter.core.pipeline.normalize()`. No canonical-state writes (ADR-0008); live ingestion deferred per `auth.md`. Both are local-artifact (T0) sources — neither tool exposes a public read API.

## Connectors

| Connector | Surface | Source | Trust tier |
|---|---|---|---|
| **continue** (pkg `continue_dev`) | `parse_event` | Continue dev-data JSONL event (versioned schema) | T0 |
| **aider** | `parse_commit` | Aider `(aider)` git-commit attribution | T0 |

- **Continue**: prompt/completion → excerpt with a `continue {name}` terminal floor; `name`→kind; model/schema→metadata. Package is `continue_dev` because `continue` is a Python keyword; `source_id` is `"continue"`.
- **Aider**: commit subject → excerpt with an `aider-commit` floor; `_attributed_by` classifies the attribution channel (author / committer / co-author). Git attribution is the only stable, documented, code-free Aider surface; the unversioned transcript file is deferred.

Both excerpt paths end in a guaranteed non-empty terminal literal (`evidence_excerpt_blank` contract), and both parsers are type-defensive against version-skewed records.

## Governance (full QOR cycle)

Research → plan → independent audit (PASS) → implement → independent review → substantiate, sealed at META_LEDGER Entries #27–#29. Both tools added to `INTEGRATION_CANDIDATE_CATALOG` (§6.1, P1, T0); `FEATURE_INDEX` rows `FX-CONTINUE-001` / `FX-AIDER-001` verified (21 total). SHADOW_GENOME SG-2026-06-04-H/I.

## Verification

- `pytest adapter/core/tests connectors` — **136 passed**
- `ruff` + `mypy` — clean (67 files)
- `python scripts/governance_gate.py` — ledger chain + FEATURE_INDEX verified
- Independent review (objective observer + devil's advocate): **2 blockers + 3 non-blocking** found; both blockers (wrong-typed-

prs.title: mill: Add Python syntax-linter guard to `write_file` and `edit_file` in `fs_tools.py` (20260528T071800Z-add-python-syntax-linter-guard-to-write--83b1)
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Source

SWE-agent's Agent-Computer Interface (ACI) design, principle #1: "A linter runs when an edit command is issued and blocks the edit if the code isn't syntactically correct" (https://swe-agent.com/latest/background/aci/). Aider implements the same pattern via `--auto-lint` / `--lint-cmd`: after each AI edit, it runs configured linters and feeds error output back to the LLM for correction before proceeding (https://aider.chat/docs/usage/lint-test.html).

## Current gap

`write_file` and `edit_file` in `src/robotsix_mill/agents/fs_tools.py` write content to disk with no syntax validation at all. The implement stage's fix loop (implement → test → retry) catches broken code only after the full test suite runs, which is expensive in both time and LLM tokens. A trivial syntax error (missing colon, unmatched paren) wastes an entire test cycle.

## Proposed change

Add an optional, lightweight syntax check to `write_file` and `edit_file` that runs only for `.py` files (detected via file extension). Use `ast.parse(content)` or `compile(content, path, 'exec')` to validate syntax before writing to disk. If the syntax check fails, return a descriptive error with the line number and message — the agent can then correct the edit and retry without ever hitting the test gate.

Key design decisions:
- **Opt-in by extension**: only validate `.py` files; skip `.md`, `.yaml`, `.json`, etc. (avoid false positives on non-Python content).
- **Non-blocking for non-Python**: if the repo has frontend code, a Python-only guard is still a strict improvement over no guard at all.
- **Configurable off-switch**: add a `MILL_LINT_ON_EDIT=false` env var to disable the guard for repos where it causes problems.
- **Do NOT run linters** (ruff, pylint): those require the full module graph and can fail on work-in-progress code. Stick to `ast.parse` / `compile` which only checks syntax, not semantics.

## Implementation sketch

In `build_fs_tools()` (fs_tools.py), after constructing the `write_f

prs.title: chore(audit): directory freshness audit 2026-06-07 — batch 1 (aider → crewai)
prs.bodyExcerpt:
## Directory Audit — 2026-06-07

Verified 12 entries with the oldest `lastVerifiedAt` (all dated 2026-06-01):
`aider`, `arize-phoenix`, `arxiv-sanity`, `bolt-new`, `braintrust`, `brave-search-api`, `cartesia`, `chroma`, `claude-code`, `cline`, `cloudflare`, `crewai`.

Each was checked against its official site and/or GitHub repo for: link health, pricing tier, `openSource` correctness, `deployment`, platforms, model support, and insight accuracy.

---

## Re-verified — no change (lastVerifiedAt bumped)

| Entry | Verified against | Result |
|---|---|---|
| aider | aider.chat | ✅ Still open-source, byo-key, local; 44k stars, active |
| bolt-new | bolt.new | ✅ Still freemium, cloud |
| braintrust | braintrust.dev/pricing | ✅ Still freemium (Starter $0/mo confirmed) |
| brave-search-api | brave.com/search/api | ✅ Still freemium ($5 free credits/mo) |
| cartesia | cartesia.ai/pricing | ✅ Still freemium (20K credits/mo free tier) |
| chroma | trychroma.com | ✅ Still open-source Apache 2.0, still freemium |
| claude-code | claude.com/claude-code | ✅ Still live; note: docs URL now redirects to code.claude.com/docs (see below) |
| cline | cline.bot | ✅ Still open-source Apache 2.0, still byo-key |
| cloudflare | cloudflare.com/developer-platform | ✅ Still freemium with generous free tiers |
| crewai | github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI | ✅ Still MIT licensed, open-source, active |

---

## Substantive fixes

### 1. `arize-phoenix` — `openSource: true` → `false`

**Source:** [github.com/Arize-ai/phoenix](https://github.com/Arize-ai/phoenix)

Phoenix is licensed under **Elastic License 2.0 (ELv2)**, which is source-available but explicitly not OSI-approved open-source (per CLAUDE.md: "source-available ≠ OSI"). The repo states: _"This software is licensed under the terms of the Elastic License 2.0 (ELv2)."_

Changes made:
- `openSource: true` → `openSource: false`
- Tagline: removed "Open-source" prefix (was factually incorrect)
- Description: removed "open-source observability" phra

prs.title: Add installer endpoints used by harbor agents and benchmarks
prs.bodyExcerpt:
Searched [harbor](https://github.com/harbor-framework/harbor)'s source for sandbox-side `curl`/`wget` endpoints; ten hosts referenced from agent installers and adapter Dockerfiles aren't currently whitelisted, so each RSTs on Tier 1/2 (`curl: (35) Connection reset by peer`).

## Agent installers

| domain | used by |
|---|---|
| `aider.chat` | aider |
| `cursor.com` | cursor-cli (apex; `*.cursor.com` already there) |
| `gh.io` | github copilot-cli (URL shortener → `raw.githubusercontent.com`, target already covered) |
| `acli.atlassian.com` | rovodev-cli (GPG key + apt repo) |

## Adapter Dockerfiles

| domain | used by |
|---|---|
| `deb.nodesource.com` | adebench, aider_polyglot, ds1000 (apex `nodesource.com` already there) |
| `golang.org` | aider_polyglot (apex; `*.golang.org` already there) |
| `dl.google.com` | aider_polyglot (302 target of `golang.org/dl/...`) |
| `sh.rustup.rs` | aider_polyglot (apex `rustup.rs` already there) |
| `cmake.org` | featbench |
| `repo.anaconda.com` | scienceagentbench, replicationbench (Miniconda installer) |

## Repro

In a `debian:bookworm-slim` Tier 1/2 sandbox:

```
apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl
curl -fsSL https://<host>/<path> -o /dev/null
# → curl: (35) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer
```

All ten verified failing on 2026-04-29; same URLs work on Tier 3/4.
